steps.title: Test the timing hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that municipalities should respond more or offer more incentives when the expansion announcement would come before an election. Evidence weakening it should show little or no difference between the before- and after-election conditions. Because the claim is comparative, the strongest counterevidence must compare the two timing groups.

steps.title: Reject one-sided data
steps.content: A describes only pre-election nonresponse, C describes only pre-election incentives among respondents, and D describes only the post-election group. The weakening evidence must compare both timing groups directly. The answer is B.
